title: NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking
exclusions: Does not replace signed audit opinions, legal review of assessment scope, or statutory filings; does not interpret whether a given assessment satisfies national transposition.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.43` output, linked ServiceNow records in `index=itsm`, supporting `pentest:report` events, and archived dashboard export in `index=audit_evidence`.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(f) requires policies to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures. The UC tracks completion status of annual security assessments (penetration tests, audits, red-team exercises). It proves assessments were scheduled and completed but cannot evaluate whether the assessment scope was sufficient, hence partial.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) (Policies and procedures effectiveness) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.43: NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.43' running on nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv, index=itsm (GRC tasks), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Load fixture data with `next_assessment_due` ninety days in the past and no matching `closed_at` in `index=itsm`; the search must list that `legal_entity_id` with `overdue=1` and blank `last_closed`, proving detection of a missed annual cycle.
controlTest.negativeScenario: For an entity whose register date is next quarter and whose latest `snow:sn_grc_policy` event shows `closed_at` after the previous due date, the search returns zero rows because `overdue` evaluates to zero after the threshold logic.

dataSources: `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv` (fields `legal_entity_id`, `assessment_type`, `next_assessment_due`); `index=pentest` `sourcetype=pentest:report` with `report_id`, `closed_date`, `risk_rating`; `index=vuln` for open exposure context; `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` or task variants after Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)).
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)); optional HTTP Event Collector collectors for `pentest:report` JSON.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_assessment_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=itsm (sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy OR sourcetype=snow:task)
      short_description="*NIS2*annual*assessment*" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(closed_at) as last_closed latest(state) as ticket_state by company
    | rename company as legal_entity_id
]
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=pentest sourcetype=pentest:report earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(closed_date) as last_pen_report by entity
    | rename entity as legal_entity_id
]
| where overdue=1
| lookup nis2_assessment_kfp_exceptions legal_entity_id OUTPUT exception_until
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table legal_entity_id, assessment_type, next_assessment_due, last_closed, last_pen_report, overdue
description: Compares each NIS2-scoped legal entity’s next due date from the assessment register with the latest closed ServiceNow GRC ticket and optional penetration-test report timestamps so overdue or unsigned annual work surfaces before the board pack is written.
value: CFO, legal, and the CISO office receive a single pane showing which entities risk missing independent assurance cycles, which makes budget and third-party sourcing decisions defensible before external audit or supervisory dialogue.
implementation: (1) Populate `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv` from the authoritative entity list. (2) Standardise ticket titles with “NIS2 annual assessment”. (3) Ingest pentest summaries into `index=pentest`. (4) Schedule weekly; notify entity owners when `overdue=1`. (5) Archive dashboards to `index=audit_evidence` after sign-off.
visualization: Table of overdue entities, timeline of `last_closed`, bar chart of `assessment_type`.

references.title: Splunk Docs — HTTP Event Collector setup

knownFalsePositives:
• **M&A entity still mapped to legacy company code** — Distinguish: ServiceNow `company` differs from `legal_entity_id`. Suppress: fix lookup mapping table; short-term allowance via `nis2_assessment_kfp_exceptions.csv`.
• **Assessment completed but ticket title nonstandard** — Distinguish: pentest index shows `closed_date` within SLA while ITSM row missing. Suppress: add alternate `short_description` pattern or rely on `pentest:report` join only after SME validates field parity.
• **Regulator-approved extension on file** — Distinguish: GRC attachment references written deferral. Suppress: mark exception register row `board_approved=true` with `exception_until` date.
• **Year-end holiday blackout** — Distinguish: assessments always close first January week. Suppress: time-bound filter `where date_month!=12 OR date_mday>15` on alerting schedule or extend due dates in lookup.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, GRC assessment module data)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (HEC for external assessor report metadata)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, optional compliance dashboard integration)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow" | table label version`.
• **Indexes**: `itsm`, `pentest` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_assessment_kfp_exceptions`, `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_assessment_kfp_exceptions','nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv')`.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.21(2)(f)`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) (Policies and procedures effectiveness) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.43: NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928), landing events in `itsm`, `pentest` under `snow:sn_grc_policy`, `snow:task`, `pentest:report`. REST-polled ticket tables produce 500-5000 records per sync per table; at 300s interval expect ~3-8 GB/month for a mid-size service desk.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > ServiceNow Service Account. A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[snow://{instance_slug}]
url = https://<instance>.service-now.com
table = {table_name}
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = {sourcetype}
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `number, state, priority, assignment_group, opened_at, closed_at, sys_updated_on, caller_id, category, subcategory`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=itsm earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Populate `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv` from the authoritative entity list. The register referenced here is primary evidence — treat changes to it with the same discipline as code changes.

• **Hint 2:** Standardise ticket titles with “NIS2 annual assessment”. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** Ingest pentest summaries into `index=pentest`. Ingestion SLA drift is usually invisible until it breaks something — monitor the feed's freshness with a companion search that alerts when the newest event is older than the expected cadence.

• **Hint 4:** Schedule weekly; notify entity owners when `overdue=1`. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** Archive dashboards to `index=audit_evidence` after sign-off. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking," the aggregation produces the risk posture — the per-row values should reconcile to the organisation's risk register entries, not stand alone as engineering metrics.

This search begins with `| inputlookup nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv` — the base dataset is a CSV/KV Store lookup, not live event data. This inverts the usual Splunk assumption: the detection does not stream from an index, it iterates a register. The register itself is the authoritative source of truth (contracts, policies, sign-offs, attestations) and the search joins it against indexed events to find gaps.

Lookup authority: treat `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv` as a primary compliance artifact. Its contents ARE the answer the regulator will ask for ('show me your vendor list,' 'show me your CDE inventory,' 'show me your policy exceptions'). Implement the following controls on the lookup itself, not just the search:

• Version the CSV in a git repository with a mandatory pull-request review for every change.
• Maintain a signed-record trail: who changed what, when, and with what approval. A lookup with a silent edit history is an audit liability.
• Automate a nightly diff: `| inputlookup nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv | eval hash=md5(...) | outputlookup append=true nis2_annual_assessment_register_audit.csv` — this produces an immutable record of the register's state over time.
• Lock the lookup with role-based access: only the content owner has `list_inputs` + `write_file` on the path; everyone else is read-only.

The live-data side of the join (across the last 400 days) is the detection trigger — when the register and the telemetry disagree, that's a finding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.43: NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -400d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.43,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `assessment_type`, `due_epoch`, `last_closed`, `legal_entity_id`, `next_assessment_due`, `overdue`. Run:

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table assessment_type, due_epoch, last_closed, legal_entity_id, next_assessment_due, overdue
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `nis2_assessment_kfp_exceptions`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_assessment_kfp_exceptions
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.43: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Table Of Overdue Entities (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Timeline Of `Last Closed` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Bar Chart Of `Assessment Type` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**Search returns no results when violations are expected:** Verify time-window alignment, index existence, and field extractions in that order. Most 'missing results' are simpler configuration issues before they're detection-logic issues.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We keep an organised list of when each part of the company must finish its yearly security check-up. If the date has passed and we still cannot see a completed ticket or report, we tell the owner in plain language so it can be fixed calmly.
